Video.summary: Water quality is everything—and the Star brite - SeaSafe team is back at it for the second year in a row! Led by Cory Redwine, Conservation Director of Project SeaSafe, this is the 4th Annual Clean Water Collective and it’s bigger than ever. This time, we’re in Grant, Florida, where 4 million clams are being released into the Indian River Lagoon, paired with the planting of thousands of mangroves, oyster work by CCA and trash clean ups to help restore one of Florida’s most critical ecosystems. But it’s not just about hard work—it’s about connection. With local captains, fishing legends, and the Local Knowledge crew on hand, we hit the water to show why this place is worth fighting for. From clam drops by drone, to in-water mangrove planting, to monster snook catches, this is hands-on conservation at its best. This two-part series kicks off with action, heart, and the kind of hope only a healthy waterway can bring. Don’t miss Part 1—where conservation meets community and everyone gets their hands wet.
Video.title: "SeaSafe Snook Part 1"

Video.summary: As part of this year's Star brite Sea Safe event, we hit the water to experience some of the incredible fishing the Indian River Lagoon has to offer. For Rush, that meant some serious bow time with close friend Alex Hughey - on the hunt for some of the biggest snook we've ever encountered. The bite wasn't easy, but that didn't stop Rush from smashing his personal best - not once, but twice - in one unforgettable day on the water. Don't miss this action-packed episode of the Sea Safe adventure from beautiful Sebastian, Florida!
Video.title: "SeaSafe Snook Part 2"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela dives into the heart of one of the world’s largest game reserves - the Selous. He explores the synergy between hunting and conservation that is preserving this wild paradise.
Video.title: "A Conservation Legacy"

Video.summary: PCB Fishing report author Nathan Chennaux joins Capt. Justin - scanning the beach waters for tarpon and hooks into a monster while maneuvering around storms and vacationers. Does it get to the boat?
Video.title: "Live Fishing Report"

Video.summary: Joined by PCB’s Artificial Reef Coordinator Erin Graham Capt Justin - fish previously dropped reefs before sinking the famed BLACK PEARL from iconic films right off Panama City Beach, FL.
Video.title: "Black Pearl’s Final Resting Place"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike and Dallas King take advantage of a powerful summer tide, hooking into an all-out redfish frenzy where the action never slows down.
Video.title: "King Tide"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through Georgia, where we stop in Helen, Alpharetta and Atlanta!!
Video.title: "We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"

Image.alt: "We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"
"We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"
"We Crushed Them! Fishing the HEART of Georgia (Atlanta)"

Video.summary: This episode of Huckberry Homes brings us out to the Western Slope, just a few miles outside of Vail, Colorado to visit our longtime friend and collaborator Sinuhe Xavier. When he's not traveling the world shooting commercials and making short films, Xavier's at home in his quiet, perfectly appointed retreat deep in the Rockies here he's a three hour drive from Red Rock desert and a ten minute drive from some of the best snowboarding in the world. There was plenty of magic in Sinuhe's spot, from his BMW GS to his exploration of how his love for his son shaped the design and decoration of his pad.
Video.title: "Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"

Image.alt: "Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"
"Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"
"Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike navigates South Carolina’s breathtaking marsh, seizing a brief window when the seasonal high tide floods the grass, bringing incredible fishing.
Video.title: "Flood Tide"

Video.summary: Capt. Mike heads to Venice, LA, to explore a unique bayou technique —beach fishing for redfish and black drum in the heart of the Gulf.
Video.title: "Redfish Lodge"

Video.summary: Mike tries to hold his own while fishing with Nick Fischer and Liza Fitzgerald in south seas Captiva. Good luck buddy.
Video.title: "Lil Jon"

Video.summary: Mike visits Crystal River and fishes with one of his Citrus County friends, Capt JC Livingston, where they battle the good, the bad and the ugly that comes with Tarpon fishing!
Video.title: "100 Ways To Lose A Tarpon"

Video.summary: The collaboration story between Winchester and Browning to create the groundbreaking 6.8 Western is featured. Tag along on a mule deer hunt in Utah with Chris Keefer and Winchester’s Jason Gilbertson, then follow Casey Keefer to Colorado for elk and Nebraska for whitetail where the 6.8 Western is put to the test.
Video.title: “The Western Front”

Video.summary: Blair is flats fishing off the west coast of Florida with Capt. Greg DeVault.
Video.title: "Mudboats & Spring Tides"

Video.summary: Blair is near shore fishing from Florida's Space Coast with Capt. Jim Ross.
Video.title: "Space Coast Bottle Rocket"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair and Brian Sanders and guest Capt. Garret George head way out into the gulf from everglades city Florida
Video.title: "Mayhem In The Gulf"

Video.summary: Blair and Capt Chris Broome head to the back country of Marco Island to catch fish. Who knows what they'll catch.
Video.title: "Marco-Medly"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger chase the full moon Wahoo bite fishing out of Plantation Boat Mart.
Video.title: "Wahoos on the November Full Moon"

Video.summary: Captain Scott Walker heads swordfishing out of Hawks Cay with his friend, retired WWE wrestler Travis Tomko.
Video.title: "Swordfishing with Travis Tomko"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Ariel Medero fish with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy out of Hawks Cay sampling the bottom fishing and deep dropping. In between the action they learn about the Nature Conservancy’s mission to promote sustainable and transparent fishing practices.
Video.title: "Bottom Fishing with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy"

Image.alt: "Bottom Fishing with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy"
"Bottom Fishing with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy"
"Bottom Fishing with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger break in Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible with some muttons for dinner and an epic shallow water sailfish double.
Video.title: "Dinner and a Show 2025"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fish with Daiwa’s Marc Mills out of Plantation Boat Mart trying out all of Daiwa’s new jigging equipment.
Video.title: "Jigging with Daiwa’s Marc Mills"

Video.summary: U.S. Marines combat correspondent Ken Plant tells of being captured and tortured as a POW.
Video.title: "Ken Plant"

Video.summary: The Odyssea Sportfishing family grows as Casey Villone comes aboard as Rush's new deck hand. While Rush gained a helping hand on his charters, he also scored a new "Fishing Mate" who is always game to bend a rod. With a day off and a report of Tuna offshore, the two head out to see if they can beat the sharks and land a Key West sushi dinner.
Video.title: "Fishing Mate"

Video.summary: Rush teams up with longtime friend and fellow angler Jesus Diaz for a well-earned "busman's holiday" in the beautiful waters of Key West. Even on a day off, these two can't resist seeing what's going to bite. Taking the wind and the tides into account, the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day "off" work.
Video.title: "Fishing With Zeus in Key West"

Video.summary: Afroz Shah, an Indian environmental activist and lawyer from Mumbai, is responsible for spearheading the world’s largest beach clean-up project. Shah’s commitment to spending his weekends picking up trash on Versova Beach has inspired a movement of people around the world dedicated to cleaning up their own surrounding environment.
Video.title: "From Mumbai With Love"

Video.summary: When Justin and Meredith stay positive after a series of bad breaks, it pays off in fun. If fun means fish. After all, we are in Panama City Beach.
Video.title: "Positive = Good Day"

Video.summary: Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.
Video.title: "We’re gonna need a bigger cooler."

Video.summary: Black bear conservation requires different approaches across the United States - Dan Cabela explores the reality of what is being done to protect the species in Oklahoma, Arkansas and Maine.
Video.title: "Bear Necessities"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela joins a well-known outfitter in Spain to pursue an iconic species - the ibex. Along the way, he learns about the rich Spanish hunting tradition, the care with which the practice is managed and the conservation-minded ethos of the country.
Video.title: "Spanish Icons"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela sets out to learn about the model of private ownership of wildlife in South Africa. Along the way, he participates in game capture, attends an auction and hunts to support a community.
Video.title: "Private Ownership of Wildlife"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor use Lowrance Active Target all day to find fish, first targeting bridge tarpon then finding a variety of species in Florida Bay mullet muds.
Video.title: "Bridge to Mullet Muds"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor chase Tarpon of all sizes around Florida Bay and the Everglades.
Video.title: "Tarpon Bites"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor catch spotted sea trout and jacks on the grass flats and potholes in Florida Bay.
Video.title: "Islamorada Trout Fishing"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head to the iconic Seven Mile Bridge targeting big jack crevasses and permit.
Video.title: "Seven Mile Jacks"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head offshore to the Marathon Hump, taking advantage of the 26-feet hybrid boat's versatility to find some exciting Blackfin and Skipjack Tuna fishing.
Video.title: "The Hybrid Advantage "

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by scientist Nick Haddad, who educates our hosts and the fishing world on the various descending devices used to preserve stressed catch from deeper waters as the three of them get into some fun Red Snapper fishing off the coast of Marathon, Florida in the Keys.
Video.title: "Returning 'Em Right"

Video.summary: Huckberry's flagship adventure series is back in Japan and the DIRT crew is hungry as ever for adventure. This time, they’re diving into the Kansai region—where millennia-old traditions flow from the shore of Lake Biwa to surf breaks on the coast. Chef Coleman Griffin gives them a taste of the deep cultural immersion that inspires his culinary practice, from chef knives descended from samurai swords to fermented carp that went on to spawn sushi. And turns out, the perfect meal does exist, and the crew knew it as soon as they had a bite of their final omakase.
Video.title: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Video.summary: The BaitFuel Fishing Tour kicks off it’s season in Quinte West, Ontario where the teams will fish this Largemouth Bass Only event on the water of the Bay of Quinte.
Video.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 1 - Quinte West, Ontario"

Image.alt: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 1 - Quinte West, Ontario"
BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 1 - Quinte West, Ontario"
BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 1 - Quinte West, Ontario"

Video.summary: Qualifier 2 finds the tour anglers headed to the St. Lawrence River out of Brockville, Ontario.
Video.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 2 - Brockville, Ontario"

Video.summary: This week the teams descend upon Cornwall, Ontario to fish Lake St. Francis for back to back events over the weekend.
Video.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 3 - Cornwall Ontario"

Video.summary: The second event day in Cornwall, Ontario takes place on Lake St. Francis where big rains have things stirred up for the teams.
Video.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour - "Qualifier 4 - Cornwall, Ontario"

Video.summary: The Local Knowledge crew heads to Catalina Island off the coast of Southern California to participate in the 2024 War Heroes On Water tournament. This event offers a unique opportunity for the sport fishing community to create meaningful connections with our nation's heroes while raising awareness of the challenges they sometimes face when transitioning back to civilian life. With a little bit of luck and a lot of teamwork, Ali and Rush hope to put their veterans on a Bluefin tuna bite they won't forget!
Video.title: "War Heroes On Water"

Video.summary: Rush takes up an invite from good friend and fellow guide Jared Cyr to sight fish for barracuda and bonefish in the shallow waters of the Florida Keys. Meanwhile Rush's boat takes a break from charter fishing to get a new Seakeeper RIDE system installed.
Video.title: "Flats Ride"

Video.summary: After a great trip fishing the flats, Rush returns the favor and extends an invite to friend Jarred Cyr fishing offshore. Fishing in Key West is all about the variety of species and opportunities they present. Mixing it up and taking advantage of this, the guys decide to see how many species they can catch using light spin and fly tackle offshore.
Video.title: "Trading Trips"

Video.summary: The LK crew returns to Guatemala with more friends for our yearly dose of world class fishing! Year after year, Casa Vieja Lodge continues to impress with daily shots at sailfish, marlin, mahi, and tuna. Tag along on our 2nd annual group trip for a tropical fishing experience like no other!
Video.title: "Return to Casa Vieja Lodge"

Video.summary: We are in Marathon, the heart of the Florida Keys, where crystal-clear waters meet charming island vibes. Known for its pristine dive sites, rich marine life, and welcoming locals, Marathon offers some of the best underwater adventures in the world.
Video.title: "Marathon"

Video.summary: As a child, Piotr Chara spent countless hours on the Oder River in Poland, fishing with his father and wondering at the wildlife; as he grew, Piotr noticed a decline in the river’s bird species and decided to take action. Now an award-winning photographer - and a father himself - Piotr runs an organization that builds floating nests for the birds and restores the rapidly receding wetlands.
Video.title: "Wings Of The Oder"
